page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is configured with 192 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong to top-tier performance across multi-threaded compute and memory operations. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, Passmark CPU scores, and bzip3 decompression. It also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in static web serving throughput, database operations, and LLM prompt processing for small models. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks rank in the top 25 percent. However, single-core stress-ng performance and Passmark memory scores fall into the average category.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-96 offers high compute density by allocating 96 vCPUs alongside a lean 192 GB memory footprint, minimizing overhead for workloads that do not require massive RAM arrays. The primary tradeoff is the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, which limits its suitability for heavy storage-bound databases or massive GPU-accelerated deep learning training. It is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput web serving, text and image processing, and CPU-based LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 48 physical cores with hyperthreading and a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, focusing its resource profile on high-density CPU compute. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, cryptography, and static web serving, alongside strong memory bandwidth. While single-core stress-ng performance and memory scores are average, its high vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly cost-effective for compute-bound tasks. It is best suited for workloads such as software compilation, CPU-based LLM inference, high-throughput web serving, and parallel data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server with 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance, designed for high-throughput compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 features 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, optimized for high-performance compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs for demanding multi-threaded processing and CPU-based inference workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8581C processors
Top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, cryptography, and software compilation
Optimized for compute-bound tasks with a lean 2.0 GB RAM-per-core ratio
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Well-suited for CPU-based LLM inference, web serving, and parallel data processing
